title: althaea bertolae
intro: Althaea bertolae, also known as the Bertolotti marshmallow, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, soothing, and antimicrobial effects. Althaea bertolae has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from digestive discomfort. Herbal preparations of Althaea bertolae, including teas and infusions, are often used to harness its medicinal properties, while its root is also used in traditional medicine to create salves and creams for topical application.
